capability_manifest.description: Execute a PMS-ticket-controlled implementation task against a project repository.

user_facing.name: Execute approved development work
user_facing.description: Implement, test, and organize code changes only for a concrete PMS issue that a human moved into execution.

approval_hints.summary: This is not started from chat. It runs only after a PMS issue is explicitly moved into execution and may produce code changes, commits, or a pull request.

when_to_use: Use only from the PMS ticket execution service after a human moves an existing PMS issue into execution.
Use only when the runtime context contains a concrete PMS issue id, issue snapshot, approved work item, and repository binding.
when_not_to_use: Do not use for research reports, product brief or PRD drafting, architecture-only analysis, or ticket decomposition.
Do not use from Reception/chat planning, even when the user asks to implement, code, commit, open a PR, or run tests.
Do not use when the only available input is task_brief or work_item_draft_graph without a PMS issue execution context.
positive_examples: A PMS Todo issue with an acquired execution lease invokes Cortex to implement the issue in a linked repository.
negative_examples: A chat request asks to implement a feature before PMS tickets exist, or asks to start coding from a work_item_draft_graph.
routing_profile.planner_notes: High-risk repo-mutating executor. Reception planning must not select this capability. Chat development requests must first create PMS backlog issues; PMS ticket execution invokes Cortex later with a concrete issue context.

metadata.role_description: Executes approved build work against a repository, including code edits, git operations, and pull request creation.
when_to_use: Existing PMS issues that a human has moved into the execution lane and that require repository edits, GitHub operations, code generation, tests, commits, or pull requests.
PMS ticket execution requests that include a concrete issue id, issue snapshot, approved work item, and repository binding.
when_not_to_use: Initial requirements clarification, research reports, product design, or task decomposition.
Chat-originated development requests before PMS issues exist.
Answering casual questions or inspecting unrelated platform status without an active PMS ticket execution.
metadata.handoff_contract: Consumes an existing PMS issue snapshot, approved work item details, and repository context; returns implementation results, code changes, and pull request metadata.
